summary: This utilization rate is a ratio of two small, volatile numbers — vouchers available and vouchers used — so it swings widely. The 100%-to-8% collapse from FY22 to FY23 reflects application-processing delays DVS reported that year, while the recent slide toward 21% in FY26 is mostly supply-side: DVS says a much larger allocation of vouchers arrived that year than the agency's caseload could immediately use.

sources.label: DVS, Preliminary Fiscal 2026 Mayor's Management Report

oneLiner: decreasing, mostly because voucher supply outpaced DVS's caseload capacity
